terms.definition: A traditional Indian contact sport where a raider must tag opponents and return to their side while chanting 'kabaddi' in one breath.
terms.exampleUsage: The children played kabaddi in the school ground during the games period.

terms.definition: One of the oldest Indian games where two teams take turns chasing and running. Chasers sit in a row and tag runners by saying 'kho'.
terms.exampleUsage: Our school team won the kho-kho tournament at the district level.

terms.definition: A traditional game played with two sticks - a short one (gilli) and a long one (danda). The gilli is flicked into the air and hit as far as possible.
terms.exampleUsage: Grandpa told us he used to play gilli-danda with his friends every evening.

terms.definition: Something that has been done or practised for a very long time, passed down from one generation to the next.
terms.exampleUsage: Kabaddi is a traditional game that has been played in India for hundreds of years.

terms.definition: In kabaddi, the player who goes to the other team's side to tag opponents. The raider must chant 'kabaddi' continuously without taking a breath.
terms.exampleUsage: The raider touched three players and scored three points for her team.

terms.definition: A game where players toss a small stone into numbered squares drawn on the ground and hop through them on one foot. Called stapu or kith-kith in Hindi.
terms.exampleUsage: The girls drew hopscotch squares with chalk and played during lunch break.

terms.definition: A wooden spinning top that is spun using a string wound around it. Players compete to see whose top spins the longest.
terms.exampleUsage: The colourful lattoo spun so fast that it looked like a blur!

terms.definition: When a group of people work together to achieve a goal. In games, teamwork means cooperating with your teammates to win.
terms.exampleUsage: Good teamwork helped our kabaddi team win the match.

terms.definition: Playing a game honestly and following the rules without cheating. Being a good sport whether you win or lose.
terms.exampleUsage: Our teacher always reminds us that fair play is more important than winning.

terms.definition: Being healthy and strong because of regular exercise and movement. Traditional games help children stay physically fit.
terms.exampleUsage: Playing outdoor games every day improves our physical fitness.
